covered.title: Accidental Liquid Damage on iPhone 15 Pro
covered.description: The customer’s iPhone 15 Pro suffered liquid exposure when dropped into water, causing the device to stop functioning properly within the 24-month plan term.

service_fee.notes: Standard AppleCare+ service fee for ADH claims

policy_references.snippet: Accidental damage from handling caused by an unexpected and unintentional external event (e.g., liquid contact) during normal daily usage is covered with a service fee.
The Plan does not apply to cosmetic damage that does not affect functionality; however, functional failure due to liquid exposure is covered.

covered.reasoning: Since the damage was due to liquid exposure (an unexpected external event) causing functional failure within the plan term, coverage is granted per section 3.2. Cosmetic damage exclusions in section 4.2(v) do not apply because functionality is affected. The customer must pay the $49 service fee as specified.

covered.title: Functional failure due to internal hardware defect on iPhone 13
covered.description: The iPhone 13 unexpectedly shuts down and will not power on despite sufficient battery charge. Device is within AppleCare+ coverage period and no evidence of accidental damage or external causes.

service_fee.notes: No service fee applies for hardware defects without accidental damage (Sec. 3.1).

policy_references.snippet: If during the Plan Term, a defect in materials and workmanship arises, Apple will repair or replace the Covered iPhone at no charge.
The Plan does not apply to defects caused by normal wear and tear or normal aging of the product.

covered.reasoning: The device failure is due to a defect in materials arising within the Plan Term and is not caused by normal wear and tear or aging (Section 4.2(vii)). Therefore, coverage applies per Section 3.1. No service fee applies as this is not an ADH claim.
